question.content: Lithium iron phosphate (LiFePO4) is an important cathode material for lithium-ion batteries. The crystal-cell structures relevant to this problem are given in the accompanying image. During charging, LiFePO4 releases part of its lithium and becomes Li(1-x)FePO4. Determine only the value of x. Answer format: a single numerical value; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Read the crystal occupancy pattern and compare Li-filled and Li-deficient structures.
step_1.key_point: Unit counting and vacancy fraction determine x.

step_3.content: Use LiFePO4 to Li1-xFePO4 occupancy change to solve x.
